articles.title: Curiosity Rover Detects DNA-Like Molecule in 3.5-Billion-Year-Old Martian Clay
articles.summary: NASA’s Curiosity rover has identified a nitrogen-containing molecule on Mars that resembles a DNA building block—a first for the Red Planet. The discovery, made in ancient clay in Gale Crater, suggests complex organic molecules can survive for billions of years. Scientists can’t yet determine if the molecules are signs of past life or came from meteorites.

articles.title: Wave-Particle Duality Confirmed for Antimatter in Landmark Positronium Experiment
articles.summary: In a quantum physics first, researchers have observed a beam of positronium—an exotic atom made of an electron and a positron—producing a diffraction pattern through graphene. The experiment, led by Tokyo University of Science, confirms that antimatter systems exhibit wave-like behavior, opening doors for precision tests of gravity and non-destructive material analysis.

articles.title: From Software Debugger to Math Olympiad: The Rise of Lean, the Program That Verifies Mathematical Truth
articles.summary: A new book, The Proof in the Code, chronicles how a proof assistant called Lean evolved from a Microsoft Research project into a cornerstone of modern mathematics and AI. Lean can verify complex proofs and helped train AlphaProof, an AI that earned a silver medal at the 2024 International Mathematical Olympiad.
